summary: Completed testing of ShipBot Telegram shipping label bot - both backend API and frontend admin panel. Critical bug fix (infinite recursion in safe_answer_query) verified as FIXED in both files.
critical_code_review_comments: ✅ VERIFIED: safe_answer_query function in telegram_bot_app.py (line 29) now correctly calls 'await query.answer()' instead of recursively calling itself
✅ VERIFIED: safe_answer_query function in telegram_conversation.py (line 25) now correctly calls 'await query.answer()' instead of recursively calling itself
Both files had the same infinite recursion bug that would cause stack overflow when any Telegram button was pressed - this is now FIXED
test_credentials: Admin Panel - Username: admin, Password: ShipBot2026!Secure (HTTP Basic Auth)
seed_data_creation: No test data created - used existing production data
context_for_next_testing_agent: The critical bug fix has been verified. Backend API tests are in /app/backend/tests/test_shipbot_api.py. Frontend works correctly with sidebar navigation (Dashboard, Orders, Users, Admin). Admin panel requires HTTP Basic Auth.

backend_endpoints: /api/telegram/status - Returns bot status with production_loaded flag
/api/admin/api-config - Returns environment config (requires HTTP Basic Auth)
frontend_pages: Dashboard - Shows stats (Total Orders: 69, Recent: 6, Cost: $1200.21, Labels: 63) and charts
Orders - Shows order list with tracking, carrier, route, status, pricing
Users - Shows 111 users with total balance $89.37
Admin Panel - Login works, ShipEngine API config shows Production mode selected

critical_bug_fix.issue: Infinite recursion in safe_answer_query function causing bot crashes
critical_bug_fix.status: FIXED - Both functions now call await query.answer() correctly
